items.title: Kroenig quoted in The Guardian on Rubio’s legacy
items.summary: On May 22, Atlantic Council vice president and Scowcroft Center senior director Matthew Kroenig was quoted in The Guardian, explaining that Trump's current foreign policy is not unlike that Rubio promoted on his 2016 presidential campaign. The post Kroenig quoted in The Guardian on Rubio’s legacy appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: District of Columbia National Guard Unveils Freedom 250 Patch
items.summary: District of Columbia National Guard leaders introduced a new assignment patch, honoring America's 250th birthday and recognizing the National Guard's support of key events in the nation's capital.

items.title: Coast Guard Commissions 62nd Fast Response Cutter Honoring 9/11 Hero
items.summary: The Coast Guard commissioned its newest fast response cutter, the Vincent Danz, for official entry into its service fleet during a ceremony in New York City.

items.title: Chhangani’s research featured in a Financial Times article on the uptick in transaction value settled on CIPS in March amidst the Iran war.
items.summary: Read the full article here. The post Chhangani’s research featured in a Financial Times article on the uptick in transaction value settled on CIPS in March amidst the Iran war. appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: An oil windfall will not fix Libya’s economy
items.summary: Libya's new unified budget needs transparency, accountability, and oversight to deliver for the people. The post An oil windfall will not fix Libya’s economy appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: On Memorial Day, Commander in Chief, Secretary of War, Honor Nation's Fallen
items.summary: The U.S. will soon mark its semiquincentennial — 250 years as a nation. It's a big milestone, said President Donald J. Trump, but not one that could have been achieved without more than 1 million service members having given their lives to the nation.

items.title: Gold Star Spouse Turns Grief Into Advocacy
items.summary: At Charlotte, N.C., Motor Speedway, the Coca-Cola 600 was everything it is supposed to be: horsepower, heat, noise and 600 miles of punishment. It was also remembrance, with each car carrying the name of a fallen service member.

items.title: Braw in Mirror on a Russian warship being spotted 30 miles of the UK coast
items.summary: On May 25, Transatlantic Security Initiative senior fellow Elisabeth Braw was mentioned in an article in Mirror on the Russian cruise missile ship RFN Admiral Grigorovich being spotted 30 miles off the UK coast while escorting sanctioned Russian tankers through the English Channel. The post Braw in Mirror on a Russian warship being spotted 30 miles of the UK coast appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: Braw in Future on the risk to seafarers in the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: On May 25, Transatlantic Security Initiative senior fellow Elisabeth Braw published an article in Future about the risk to seafarers in the Strait of Hormuz – and how this is holding up shipping in the strait. The post Braw in Future on the risk to seafarers in the Strait of Hormuz appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: Braw in El Pais on Russia’s hybrid activity in the Øresund Strait
items.summary: On May 25, Transatlantic Security Initiative senior fellow Elisabeth Braw was mentioned in an article in El Pais on how the Øresund Strait has become crucial to Russia’s hybrid operations. The post Braw in El Pais on Russia’s hybrid activity in the Øresund Strait appeared first on Atlantic Council .

items.title: Hegseth Tells West Point Cadets They Are Ready to Lead
items.summary: Speaking at Michie Stadium in West Point, N.Y., Secretary of War Pete Hegseth assured the 2026 U.S. Military Academy graduates that they have both his and the president's trust.

items.title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: Geo-economic resilience
items.summary: New publication in the NB8 policy brief series by Thorsteinn Kristinsson (IIA) and Ludvig Broomé (UI) Geo-economic competition and the coercive use of economic statecraft are supplanting the rules-based system of liberal economic exchange. Small economies like the NB8 states are vulnerable to this development, including the potential weaponisation of external dependencies against them. This […]

items.title: For Old Guard Soldiers, 'Flags In' Is a Personal Mission
items.summary: Soldiers assigned to the 3rd U.S. Infantry Regiment honored America's fallen heroes ahead of Memorial Day by placing a U.S. flag in front of each headstone and columbarium column at Arlington National Cemetery and at the United States Soldiers' and Airmen's Home National Cemetery.

items.title: War Department's 'Patriot Pipeline' Flows Talent to Arsenal of Freedom
items.summary: While testifying before the Senate Armed Services Committee's personnel subcommittee, Anthony J. Tata, the undersecretary of war for personnel and readiness, said the department has established Project Patriot Pipeline, an initiative to unify dozens of training and workforce development programs.

items.title: Top Special Ops Civilian Recounts Successes, Lays Out Vision for Future
items.summary: Derrick Anderson, assistant secretary of war for special operations and low-intensity conflict, closed out the Special Operations Forces Week 2026 convention by underscoring the SOF community's recent successes and articulating his plan to prioritize five areas.

items.title: Symbol of Grit Returns, 10th Mountain Division to Wear Crossed Ski Insignia
items.summary: Soldiers assigned to the 10th Mountain Division can once again wear the division's historic crossed ski insignia on their Army Green Service Uniform garrison caps, restoring a visual link to the unit's World War II roots and reinforcing the alpine spirit that resonates across the formation.

items.title: Special Ops Conference Discusses Optimizing Human Performance
items.summary: During the Special Operations Forces Week convention, experts stressed that early health checks, artificial intelligence tracking and family support are vital to protect operators before problems worsen.

items.title: Give Them Wings, Teach Them to Fly: The Making of a Phoenix Raven
items.summary: Airmen must push through a demanding 28-day qualification course to become one of the Phoenix Ravens, a force tasked with safeguarding aircraft, personnel and critical assets in austere locations where threats may be elevated or unpredictable.

items.title: FIIA takes part in European Studies Day 11 May
items.summary: The Centre for European Studies at the University of Helsinki hosts a European Studies Day on 11 May 2026. The event brings together researchers of Europe and the EU from various academic disciplines to present recent research in the field. The Finnish Institute of International Affairs will organise two panels as part of the event. […]

items.title: Deputy Director Samu Paukkunen will move to a new role starting 1 June
items.summary: Samu Paukkunen, Deputy Director of the Finnish Institute of International Affairs, has resigned from his position and will move to the energy company Fortum as of 1 June 2026. Paukkunen will take up the role of Director, geopolitics and resilience at Fortum. The vacant position will be opened for public application at a later date. […]

items.title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: The circles of security
items.summary: Russia’s war against Ukraine has profoundly worsened Europe’s security environment, but it has also spurred deeper regional cooperation. The challenge now is to ensure that this growing number of initiatives remains purposeful, adds concrete value, and strengthens both the EU and NATO.

items.title: The Nordic-Baltic Eight policy brief: How to implement a full maritime services ban on Russian oil
items.summary: The EU is considering a full maritime services ban on Russian oil. While the final proposal is still under negotiation, the measure would replace the current oil price-cap regime by prohibiting European and allied companies from providing maritime services for Russian oil exports, regardless of the sale price. The ban would cover transportation, insurance, financing, […]

items.title: FIIA welcomes invitations to public events
items.summary: To mark the Finnish Institute of International Affairs’ 65th anniversary, FIIA researchers will be taking part in public events across Finland to discuss the most important current issues in foreign and security policy. We welcome invitations to a wide range of events and venues where a discussion on foreign policy would be a good fit […]

items.title: Study: Russia’s influence activities exploited special characteristics of bilateral relationship with Finland
items.summary: A study carried out by the Finnish Institute of International Affairs together with the University of Helsinki and the University of Tampere examined Russia’s full-spectrum influence activities directed at Finland since the 2000s and their effects on Finnish society and decision-making. The government-funded research project is based on the Government Programme and a description of […]

items.title: FIIA introduces programme period 2026–28
items.summary: The new three-year research programme period of the Finnish Institute of International Affairs has begun on 1 January 2026, also marking the start of FIIA’s 65th anniversary year. The four research programmes of the previous programme period will continue their work, with some changes in their names and some new thematic emphases. The programmes are: […]

items.title: President of the Republic of Finland awards Juha Jokela the honour of Knight of the Order of the White Rose of Finland
items.summary: President of the Republic of Finland Alexander Stubb has decided to award Juha Jokela, Programme Director at FIIA, the honour of Knight of the Order of the White Rose of Finland. Jokela directs The European Union and Strategic Competition research programme. Each year on Finland’s independent day, the decorations of the Orders of the White Rose of […]
